title: SYN Flood Attack
description: A high-volume TCP SYN packet flood aimed at exhausting server connection tables.
mitigation_steps: Implement rate limiting/throttling on SYN packets at the edge firewall or router.
Enable SYN cookies on affected servers to protect against half-open connections.
Block or rate-limit traffic from suspicious source IPs using access control lists.
Monitor for unusual spikes in SYN packets and correlate with other anomaly indicators.

title: Data Exfiltration
description: Unusually large or unauthorized data transfers that may indicate sensitive data is being removed from the network.
mitigation_steps: Identify the source and destination endpoints involved in the transfer and isolate the affected systems.
Review user and service account activity for privilege escalation or misuse.
Implement data loss prevention (DLP) policies and monitor for sensitive file transfers.
Block or rate-limit suspicious destinations and enforce strict egress filtering.

title: Suspicious Protocol Usage
description: Use of non-standard or unexpected protocols that may indicate tunneling, covert channels, or unauthorized services.
mitigation_steps: Identify which hosts are using the suspicious protocol and determine if it is allowed by policy.
Block or restrict the protocol at network boundaries if it is not required.
Conduct packet inspection to validate whether the traffic is legitimate or encoded/masked.
Update IDS/IPS signatures and monitoring rules to detect further usage of the protocol.
